threshold_notes: Second pair (Jd) on K-river is below threshold at 70% even with the 7h partial blocker, given that the turn check reduced the bluff pool the 7 was meant to cover.
In both the turn-check and turn-barrel scenarios, second pair with this blocker falls short of calling threshold.
blocker_notes: 7h reduces T7h and 76h combos from BTN's range — roughly 2-4 combos removed.
After a turn check-through, many T7/76 hands have been filtered out of BTN's river betting range. The 7 blocker's effectiveness shrinks proportionally.

what_changed_by_street.detail: 50% c-bet on J95 is a standard value bet. Keeps a wide range including Jx, sets, and over-pairs.
Turn check on 8s — the key filter. Removes BTN's natural barrel range (KJ, QJ, draws that want to charge) and leaves slow-played value plus air. This is the line that determines the blocker analysis.
Ks activates Kx two-pair combos (KJ, K9, K8) and serves as a story card for pure air bluffs. BTN's range is narrower than a barrel-barrel-barrel sequence but not bluff-empty.

coaching_context.follow_up: Practice blocker analysis with and without a turn barrel: the same blocker can have different values depending on whether the corresponding draw hands were filtered by the turn action.

title: Q83 BB — Multi-Step: Same Bluff-Catcher, Different River Threshold by Turn Line
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP, 100bb effective. Board: Qd 8h 3c | 5s | Qh. You hold Ac 3d (bottom pair + ace). Evaluate the same river bet across two different turn lines to see how the threshold changes.

options.label: Call
Fold

answer.explanation: Hero has Ac3d — bottom pair (3s) and an ace. On Qd8h3c|5s|Qh, the board's Q paired on the river. BTN c-bet small (40%) on the flop and checked back the blank 5s turn. The turn check is the pivotal signal: on a Q-high board, BTN's natural barrel hands (AQ, KQ for strong Q-x) would fire the turn to protect equity or build the pot. The blank 5s invited a barrel and BTN declined. This removes most clean AQ/KQ from BTN's river betting range. What arrives: slow-played sets (88, 33), AQ that trapped, and delayed float bluffs (J9, T9, backdoor-equity hands that missed). The Qh pairs the top card, concentrating value for BTN into genuine Q holdings. But the turn check substantially shrunk that Q-value pool. Hero's Ac3d has: bottom pair (3s) on the board, an ace that blocks some AQ combos, and is above threshold to call a 60% bet given the inflated delayed-bluff density after the turn check-through.

diagnostic_prompts.prompt: Hero has bottom pair and an ace on a paired-Q river. How does the turn check-through justify a call at 60%?
diagnostic_prompts.concept: turn check-through inflates bluff density on paired rivers
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: BTN's turn check removes AQ/KQ (which would barrel for protection or value), leaving the river range inflated with delayed floats. Against that inflated bluff pool, bottom pair + A kicker is above threshold at a moderate river size.

options.label: Bottom pair is always below threshold against a bet on a paired-Q river — fold regardless of turn line.
BTN's turn check removed AQ/KQ barrels, inflating the delayed float range enough that bottom pair + ace kicker sits above threshold at 60% pot.
The ace blocker alone justifies the call — it reduces AQ significantly.

steps.prompt: SCENARIO A: Both checked the 5s turn. BTN bets 60% on river Qh. You hold Ac3d (bottom pair). Call or fold?

options.label: Call
Fold

answer.explanation: Turn check-through on a Q-high board removed AQ/KQ barrels and inflated the delayed float range. River Qh pairs the top, but BTN's Q value is thinner than it looks after not barreling. Hero's Ac3d: bottom pair (3s) + ace blocker (reduces AQ by one combo). The bluff density from the check-through is high enough that bottom pair + A is above threshold at 60%.

steps.prompt: SCENARIO B: BTN barreled 60% on the 5s turn instead. You called. Same Qh river, BTN bets 60%. Same hand Ac3d. Call or fold now?

options.label: Fold
Call — turn barrel still leaves enough bluffs

answer.explanation: When BTN barreled the 5s turn, their range was filtered: weaker hands without equity folded the turn. The remaining river range after a turn barrel on a Q-high board contains concentrated Qx value (AQ, KQ) and strong pocket pairs. The same 60% river bet now represents a much more value-heavy range. Bottom pair with an ace kicker cannot meet the calling threshold against a concentrated double-barrel range — fold.

coaching_context.key_concept: The same combo (bottom pair + A) has different river thresholds depending entirely on whether the turn was barreled or checked. The turn action is the decisive variable — not the river card, not the sizing, not the hand strength in isolation.
common_mistakes: Evaluating bottom pair against a paired-river bet without considering what the turn action tells you about the bluff density of BTN's range.
Treating 'bottom pair' as universally a fold on paired rivers, ignoring that the turn check-through changes the ratio of bluffs to value in BTN's range.

combos: AQ, KQ (check-through scenario: reduced presence; barrel scenario: full presence)

value_buckets.label: QQ (the nut slow-play) — rare but present
combos: QQ (the nut slow-play) — rare but present

bluff_buckets.label: J9, T9, K9 type gut-shot or backdoor hands that missed and fire the Q as a delayed bluff — present in check-through scenario, mostly absent after turn barrel
combos: J9, T9, K9 type gut-shot or backdoor hands that missed and fire the Q as a delayed bluff — present in check-through scenario, mostly absent after turn barrel

bluff_buckets.label: Ac-x hands that had backdoor draws and now fire Q river with ace-high narrative
combos: Ac-x hands that had backdoor draws and now fire Q river with ace-high narrative

threshold_notes: At 60% pot after a turn check-through: bottom pair + ace kicker is above threshold because the delayed float range is large relative to thin Q-x value.
At 60% pot after a turn barrel: bottom pair is below threshold because the barrel filtered out bluffs and concentrated Q-x value.
blocker_notes: Ac reduces AQ combos for BTN by about 3 combos (Ac + Qx = combos removed). This is a meaningful contribution to the call in scenario A.
In scenario B (turn barrel), the Ac blocker still removes 3 AQ combos, but the remaining range is so value-concentrated that the reduction isn't enough.

what_changed_by_street.detail: 40% small c-bet on Q83 is a range bet — wide range, low commitment.
The turn action is the pivotal variable: check-through inflates the delayed bluff pool; barrel filters it to value. The same river decision has a different answer depending solely on this one action.
Qh pairs the top. In the check-through scenario, BTN's Q-x value is thinner and the bluff pool is large. In the barrel scenario, Q-x value is concentrated and bluffs are mostly gone.

coaching_context.follow_up: Practice the concept that the same hand has different river thresholds based on turn aggression patterns. Build a habit: before evaluating a river call, reconstruct the turn action and its effect on the bluff-to-value ratio.

title: K74 BB — Multi-Step: Probe Called, River Sizing Changes Pool-Adjusted Threshold
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP, 100bb effective. BTN c-bets 40% on Kd 7c 4h, you call. Turn 9s. You probe 40% pot. BTN calls. River 3d — brick. You check. Evaluate two river sizings from BTN.

options.label: Fold
Call

answer.explanation: Hero probed the 9s turn with top pair (Ks6d on Kd7c4h). The probe was reasonable — BTN c-bet small and hero wanted to protect top pair against a board that picks up straight equity with a 9 (8-9-T draws, 9-8-7 draws). BTN CALLED the probe. A probe-call on a K-7-4-9 board means BTN has hands that can comfortably call: Kx with strong kickers (KQ, KJ, KT all beat K6), 77/44 (sets), 97s type two pair, and floats with strong equity (T8, 86, QJ with straight draws). On the 3d river (pure brick), BTN fires 50% pot. This 50% bet after calling a probe is concerning: BTN's probe-calling range includes Kx value that now wants to extract value on the brick river, sets that slowplayed through the probe, and straight completions that still have strong hands. Hero's Ks6d = top pair with a 6 kicker. The K blocker (Ks) removes some BTN Kx combos — specifically KsXx is blocked (BTN can't have Ks). But KQ, KJ, KT are each only reduced by the Ks component: KsQ, KsJ, KsT are removed (1 combo each), leaving Kc/Kh/Kd versions. Against this range, K6 kicker is near or below threshold.

C.explanation: Aggressive recreational players call probes with much wider ranges — 97, pair+draw, even stubborn under-pairs. Against Pool C, BTN's probe-calling range is diluted with these weaker hands, and their 50% river bet is not exclusively value. Against Pool C at 50% pot, the bluff density from a wider probe-calling range is sufficient for top pair K6 to call.

diagnostic_prompts.prompt: BB probed the turn, BTN called. River is a brick. What does the probe-call dynamic tell you about BTN's river betting range?
diagnostic_prompts.concept: probe-call range influences river threshold
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: BTN calling a probe on a K74-9 board signals hands that are either ahead of K6 kicker (KQ, KJ, sets) or have strong equity draws (T8, 86 for straights). On a brick river, the straight draws mostly missed and BTN's betting range is concentrated toward the made hands that called the probe — value-heavy in baseline pools.

options.label: The probe folded out strong hands, so BTN's calling range is weaker and the river bet is bluff-heavy.
BTN calling the probe on K74-9 concentrates the range around Kx (better kicker), sets, and two-pair hands — all ahead of K6. River bet from this range is value-leaning in baseline and passive pools.
The probe-call doesn't matter — river bet sizing is the only input.

steps.prompt: SCENARIO A: BTN bets 50% pot on the brick river 3d after calling your probe. You hold Ks6d (top pair). Call or fold?

options.label: Fold — probe-call range is too strong
Call — 50% is too small to be purely value

answer.explanation: BTN calling a probe on K74-9 concentrates their range around hands beating K6 kicker. At 50% pot, the bet may include some thin value and possibly a few stubborn floats, but in baseline live cash, the probe-call filtered out weak hands. K6 kicker (top pair, weak kicker) is below threshold against this range. Fold in baseline and passive pools; call against Pool C where the probe-calling range is wider.

steps.prompt: SCENARIO B: Same hand, same line, but BTN bets 100% pot instead. You hold Ks6d. Does the larger size change anything?

options.label: Fold — large bet after probe-call is even more value-heavy
Call — large size means more bluffs in the range

answer.explanation: At 100% pot, BTN's bet becomes more polar but the probe-call dynamic means there are few bluffs left in the range. Live players who call a probe and then fire pot-sized river bets are almost exclusively holding strong made hands (KQ, KJ, sets). The polarization argument for calling large bets applies when the bluff pool is large — but the probe-call already filtered that pool significantly. K6 kicker is even further below threshold at 100% than at 50%. The larger size confirms value, not bluffs, in this line.

coaching_context.key_concept: After a turn probe that was called, the river threshold shifts toward folding in baseline and passive pools because the probe-call filtered BTN's range toward hands that beat top pair weak kicker. The pool-adjusted answer for Pool C at small sizes is a call — but as size increases, even Pool C's range becomes value-heavy.
common_mistakes: Treating top pair as a standard call after probing and getting called, without recognizing that the probe-call confirmed a stronger range for BTN than a check-check turn would have.
Thinking larger river bets always mean more bluffs — on probe-call lines, larger bets typically mean more value, not more bluffs.
