title: T8 BB - Turn Check-Through Unlocks Q River Call; Punish Delayed Overbluff
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP. BTN c-bets 33% on Th 9h 4d, then checks back the Kc turn after you check. River Qs. You check. BTN bets 75% pot. You hold Tc 8h. The turn check-through is the reason this is a call - if BTN had bet the K turn and you called, the Q river would flip this to a fold.

options.label: Fold
Call
Raise

answer.explanation: The Kc turn check-through is the controlling event. BTN checked back a K on a connected T-9-4 board - this preserves the wide delayed-float range: K-high air with backdoor outs, J-x floats that use K-Q as scare cards, missed straight draw hands that now fire river as bluffs. The Qs river is a natural bluff trigger for those hands (KJ got two pair, KT got two pair, QJ got a pair, Q-high air picked up a top-card narrative). Tc8h is top pair with an 8 kicker - low in the defending range, but still above threshold because the check-through preserved enough bluff density. The flip: if BTN had bet the Kc turn and BB called, BTN's range condenses to AK, KT, KQ, KK, and strong draws. The Qs river improves much of that range (KQ two pair, QQ, etc.) and makes Tc8h a clear fold.

A.explanation: This is a thin call against competent regulars. T8 is low in the defending range and the turn check on a connected board could hide trapping strength. Call, but acknowledge the thinness.

B.explanation: Passive live players check back turns to protect and then bet rivers with strong hands. Against a passive pool, T8 is too thin - a fold is appropriate.

C.explanation: Aggressive recreationals use K and Q as scare cards on delayed lines. T8 is a clear call in an aggro-rec pool.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: K turn check-through creates Q river bluff space
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: Why does BTN checking back the Kc on a T-9-4-K board matter so much for calling the Qs river?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: A K-turn barrel would condense BTN's range toward AK, KT, KQ, KK - hands that improve substantially on the Qs river. The turn check preserves a wide range including K-high floats, J-x with no board commitment, and missed backdoor draws. Those hands use the Qs as a natural scare-card bluff trigger. T8 beats all of them and is above threshold.

options.label: The K is just a card. Checking or betting the K turn doesn't change the Q river problem much.
The K check-through keeps in K-high air, J-floats, and missed draws. Those hands bluff the Qs naturally. A barrel would have replaced them with value, making T8 a fold.
The Q river is too dangerous for one pair regardless of the turn check.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: how K-turn bet-call flips the river answer
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: If BTN had bet 55% on the Kc turn and you called, how does the Qs river change for Tc8h?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: A bet-call on the Kc turn concentrates BTN's range toward value: AK, KT, KQ, KK, and strong draws. The Qs river improves a significant portion (KQ makes two pair, QQ, KT makes two pair with a Q kicker). Tc8h is now below threshold because the bluff fraction is thin and the value fraction got stronger.

options.label: T8 calls in both scenarios. One pair always has calling equity after a check-through.
After a K-turn bet-call, BTN's value region is heavy with hands the Q river improves. T8 falls well below threshold.
Raise the river - T8 is strong enough to turn into a bluff here.

coaching_context.key_concept: Turn check-through on a connected board is the reason T8 can call a Q river. The check preserved bluff density; a barrel would have destroyed it.
common_mistakes: Folding T8 to a Q river bet without crediting the turn check-through for preserving a real bluff constituency.
Treating one pair as auto-fold on scare rivers without carrying the turn filter forward.

value_buckets.label: Value that survived the turn check
value_buckets.note: Sets and two pair that checked the K turn with trapping intent. Low frequency but present.
value_buckets.frequency_hint: Sparse - not the dominant part of BTN's check-through range

value_buckets.note: Some of these existed in BTN's check-through range and improved on Qs. Not dominant after check-through.
value_buckets.frequency_hint: Low to moderate

bluff_buckets.label: K-high and Q-high delayed floats
bluff_buckets.note: These check back K turn and fire Q river as a scare-card bluff. Core of the check-through bluff region.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: Primary bluff population in check-through line

bluff_buckets.label: Missed straight draws and backdoors
bluff_buckets.note: Had equity on flop and turn, miss the Qs, fire as bluffs.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: Substantial

bluff_catchers.label: T8 and similar - thin but above threshold
bluff_catchers.note: Low in the defending range but above threshold because the check-through preserved real bluff density.
bluff_catchers.frequency_hint: Call thinly - acknowledge the pool dependence

threshold_notes: Tc8h is a thin call and is pool-dependent.
The turn check-through is the enabling condition for the call to be correct.
Against passive pools, T8 can fold because the check-through does not preserve enough bluffs in those pool tendencies.
blocker_notes: Tc blocks TT (sets) and T9s combos, reducing some of BTN's value combos.
The Tc blocker provides a modest positive effect - reduces a few two-pair combinations.

hero_hand_bucket.label: One pair - line-dependent thin call
hero_hand_bucket.summary: CALL after check-through (thin); FOLD after K turn bet-call
hero_hand_bucket.note: This drill targets the player who reflexively folds Q-river bets without checking the turn filter.

what_changed_by_street.detail: 33% c-bet on T-9-4 connected board. Wide range stays in - BTN has the full set of value, semi-bluffs, and floats.

Kc arrives and BTN checks back. This is the controlling event. The full delayed range survives: K-high air, J-x floats, missed straight draws with 4-6 outs. No condensation toward value. The check-through is what makes T8 a profitable call on the next street.
Qs fires the natural delayed-float bluff trigger. KJ, KT, QJ, and Q-high hands use the scare cards to represent two-pair or big pairs. T8 beats all of those and should call.

coaching_context.follow_up: Study the distribution of BTN's check-back hands on K-over-connected-boards. What fraction trap, what fraction float, what fraction have no plan?

title: J9 BB - Trips on Paired River: Sizing Uncertainty Is Non-Decisive
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP. BTN c-bets 33% on Jh 7c 3d, then checks back the 2s turn. River Jd pairs the top card. You check. BTN bets - you're not exactly sure if it was around 50% or maybe 66% pot. You hold Js 9h (trips). The exact size doesn't matter. This is a clear call at any reasonable sizing in this spot.

options.label: Fold
Call
Raise

answer.explanation: Js9h is trips jacks after the Jd river, and the turn check-through preserved the full delayed-float range in BTN's range. The decisive facts are the hand strength (trips are high in range) and the line (check-through means wide bluffing range). The sizing uncertainty - 50% vs 66% - is irrelevant because trips sits far above the threshold for either size. This is a coaching target about confidence calibration: players sometimes use 'I couldn't remember the exact size' as a reason to hesitate or fold a hand that is clearly above threshold at any reasonable bet. The fuzzy size is non-decisive. Lock in the call based on the line and the hand strength.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: fuzzy sizing is non-decisive when hand is far above threshold
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: You can't remember if the bet was 50% or 66% pot. Does that uncertainty change the decision with Js9h?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: Trips jacks after a turn check-through is well above the threshold for either size. At 50%, you need to win roughly 25% of the time. At 66%, roughly 28%. Trips in a check-through range wins far more than that against BTN's value-bluff ratio. The sizing uncertainty has no material effect on the correct answer.

options.label: Yes - the exact size changes the pot odds enough to matter. I need to know the size to make the right decision.
No - trips in a check-through range is far above threshold at either size. The sizing uncertainty is non-decisive. Call without needing the exact number.
Raise to avoid the sizing ambiguity - then it doesn't matter what he bet.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: identifying the decisive vs non-decisive facts
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: In this hand, which facts are DECISIVE for the correct answer and which are FUZZY but non-decisive?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: DECISIVE: (1) the turn checked through - this preserved bluff density and set the threshold; (2) hero has trips - far above threshold. FUZZY but non-decisive: the exact river bet size (50% vs 66%). The player's memory uncertainty about the size is irrelevant when the hand is clearly above threshold.

options.label: The bet size is decisive because it determines pot odds. The turn line is less important.
Decisive: turn check-through + trips strength. Fuzzy but non-decisive: exact river bet size. Call is correct regardless of whether it was 50% or 66%.
Only the hand matters. Trips always calls on any board after any line.

coaching_context.key_concept: Sizing uncertainty is only material when you are near the bluff-catching threshold. When trips is well above threshold in a check-through line, the fuzzy size is irrelevant. Confidence calibration: do not let memory fuzz about non-decisive details create false hesitation.
common_mistakes: Using uncertainty about the bet size as a reason to hesitate or fold a hand that is clearly above threshold.
Applying excessive precision to sizing calculations when the hand strength and line already dictate the answer.

value_buckets.label: Boats and value on J-J board
value_buckets.note: Sets and two pairs that checked back turn and now bet the paired river for value.
value_buckets.frequency_hint: Low - check-through filters out some of this

bluff_buckets.label: Delayed floats and missed draws
bluff_buckets.note: These preserved their bluffing equity through the turn check and now fire the paired river as scare-card bluffs.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: Primary bluff region - high density after check-through

bluff_catchers.label: Trips - far above threshold
bluff_catchers.note: Trips are so far above the threshold that the call is correct at any reasonable river bet size.
bluff_catchers.frequency_hint: Clear call - no sizing calculation needed

threshold_notes: At 50% pot: need ~25% equity. Trips wins far more than that.
At 66% pot: need ~28% equity. Still far above.
The sizing ambiguity adds less than 3% to the required equity - irrelevant when the hand is this far above threshold.
blocker_notes: Js removes one of the remaining Jack combinations from BTN's value range (J7s, J3s are already scarce combos).
The Js blocker is mildly positive but unnecessary to justify the call.

hero_hand_bucket.label: Trips - clearly above threshold
hero_hand_bucket.summary: CALL at any reasonable size
hero_hand_bucket.note: The coaching target is recognizing when sizing uncertainty is irrelevant - not defaulting to hesitation just because you can't remember the exact number.

what_changed_by_street.detail: 33% c-bet on J-7-3. Wide range continues - all floats and value hands stay in.
2s blank, BTN checks back. The turn check-through preserves the full delayed range. This is the setup for the bluff-catch node on the paired river.
Jd pairs the top card. Hero has trips. BTN's delayed range fires the paired river as bluffs or thin value. Trips is well above threshold. The exact sizing is non-decisive.

coaching_context.follow_up: Practice identifying when a spot is so far above or below threshold that the exact bet size is irrelevant. Sizing precision matters most near the threshold - not far above or below it.

title: K3 BB - Weak Kicker Fold Stable on Paired Nine River Regardless of Whether Overbet Was 90% or 120%
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP. BTN c-bets 33% on Ks 9h 4c, then checks back the Jd turn after you check. River 9d pairs the board nine. You check. BTN overbets - you think it was somewhere around 90% or maybe 120% pot, you're not sure. You hold Kd 3c. Does the exact size matter here?

options.label: Fold
Call
Raise

answer.explanation: Kd3c is one pair with a 3 kicker on a K-9-4-J-9 board - the 9d river paired the flop nine. Even after the turn check-through preserving a meaningful bluff range, K3 sits well below the threshold for any meaningful overbet size. The paired nine also improves BTN's value range slightly (99 boats up, 9-4 makes a boat), further worsening K3's equity. At 90%, hero needs to win roughly 47% of the time. At 120%, hero needs roughly 55%. K3 is beaten by every two-pair, every set, every boat, and all of BTN's stronger K-x hands. It only beats pure bluffs. Even in a check-through line with meaningful bluff density, the ratio of bluffs to value at overbet sizing does not reach 47-55% for this combo specifically. The sizing ambiguity is non-decisive: the fold is correct at both 90% and 120%. This is a confidence calibration target - do not use 'I couldn't remember the size' as an excuse to avoid making a clear fold.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: fuzzy sizing non-decisive when hand is far below threshold
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: You remember the bet was 'big - somewhere around 90% or maybe 120%.' Does knowing the exact size change whether you call or fold with Kd3c?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: K3 is so far below threshold at both sizes that the ambiguity is irrelevant. At 90%, you need 47% equity. At 120%, you need 55%. K3 only beats air; it loses to every two-pair, straight, and stronger K-x. The bluff-to-value ratio does not reach either threshold for this specific combo, so the fold is stable regardless of the exact size.

options.label: Yes - at 90% pot it might be a thin call but at 120% it's a fold. I need the exact number.
No - K3 is below threshold at both 90% and 120%. The sizing ambiguity doesn't change the fold. Weak kickers don't cross the threshold at overbet sizing in this spot.
Overbets are often bluffs, so K3 should always call river overbets.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: identifying decisive vs non-decisive facts in a fold spot
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: What is the DECISIVE fact that makes K3 a fold here, and what is FUZZY but non-decisive?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: DECISIVE: K3 is well below threshold - it is one of the weakest top-pair combos and cannot meet the equity requirement for any reasonable overbet. FUZZY but non-decisive: whether the bet was 90% or 120%. The fold is correct at both sizes. The decisive fact is the combo's position far below threshold, not the precise sizing.

options.label: The exact bet size is the decisive fact. I need to know whether it was 90% or 120% to make the right play.
The decisive fact is that K3 is far below threshold - too weak a kicker to call any meaningful overbet. The exact size is fuzzy but non-decisive.
The J-8 on the board is the decisive factor - these straights make it impossible to call.

coaching_context.key_concept: Sizing uncertainty is only material near the threshold. K3 is so far below threshold at any overbet that the ambiguity is irrelevant. Recognizing when you are clearly below threshold and acting on it without sizing precision is a confidence calibration skill.
common_mistakes: Using 'I wasn't sure of the exact size' as a reason to hesitate or call a hand that is clearly below threshold at any reasonable bet.
Treating all overbets as polarized bluffs regardless of hand position in the defending range.

value_buckets.label: Value at K-9-4-J-8
value_buckets.note: Numerous two-pair and set combinations that BTN can hold and bet for value at overbet sizing.
value_buckets.frequency_hint: Substantial - K3 loses to all of these

value_buckets.label: Straights
value_buckets.note: QT and T7 make straights on the K-9-4-J-8 runout.
value_buckets.frequency_hint: Low but present

bluff_buckets.label: Delayed floats firing river as bluffs
bluff_buckets.note: These check back turn and fire an overbet river with a polar story. Present but not dominant enough to make K3 a call.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: Meaningful but insufficient for K3

bluff_catchers.label: K-x hands above threshold
bluff_catchers.note: Stronger kickers that can defend this overbet because they beat more of BTN's bluff combinations and are closer to threshold.

bluff_catchers.label: K3 - below threshold at any overbet
bluff_catchers.note: K3 only beats pure air. Cannot meet the equity requirement at 90% or 120%.

threshold_notes: At 90% pot: need ~47% equity. K3 does not come close.
At 120% pot: need ~55% equity. K3 is even further below.
The 10-15% difference in required equity between 90% and 120% is irrelevant when K3 is 30%+ below either threshold.
blocker_notes: Kd removes a few KK, KJ combinations from BTN's value range, but these are already low-frequency and don't move the needle for K3.

hero_hand_bucket.label: Weak top pair - clearly below threshold
hero_hand_bucket.summary: FOLD at any overbet sizing
hero_hand_bucket.note: The coaching target is comfort making this fold cleanly without needing the exact bet size.

what_changed_by_street.detail: 33% c-bet on K-9-4. Wide range stays in. BTN has the full K-x value set and all floats.

Jd arrives. BTN checks back. Turn check-through preserves delayed range including QJ and other float combos. K3 was already a weak hand but the check-through creates some bluff density that helps stronger K-x combos. K3 is too weak even with this help.

8s. BTN overbets. K3 loses to two-pairs, straights, and all strong K-x. Even the bluff density from the check-through does not make K3 profitable at overbet sizing. The size ambiguity (90% vs 120%) does not matter - fold.

coaching_context.follow_up: Find the kicker strength where K-x crosses threshold against this overbet. At what point does Kd-x become a call? KT? KJ? This is the threshold identification exercise.

title: A8 BB - Ace Blocker Hurts After Check-Through; Would Help After Bet-Call: Blocker Direction Flips
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP. BTN c-bets 33% on Kh 7s 4c, then checks back the 3d turn after you check. River As. You check. BTN bets 65% pot. You hold Ad 8c. The ace in your hand is working against you here - not for you. If the turn had been bet-called, the same card would be helping.

options.label: Fold
Call
Raise

answer.explanation: Ad8c arrives at the river with a pair of aces (As on board, Ad in hand) plus an 8 kicker. The hand is borderline - pair of aces is not trips, and on a K-7-4-3-A board with BTN's check-through range, the bluff density is real. The problem is the Ad blocker. After a turn check-through, BTN's primary bluffing region on the As river is Ax-high delayed floats: hands like A6, A5, A2, A9 that have no board connection on K-7-4-3, checked back the turn, and now fire the scare ace river as a bluff. Hero holds Ad - that card removes all of those Ad-x combinations from BTN's range, directly reducing the bluff population that makes the call correct. Ad is a negative blocker here: it blocks bluffs, not value. Without Ad, A8 might reach the threshold for a thin call. With Ad, the bluff density shrinks enough to push it below threshold. The direction flip: if BTN had bet the turn and BB called, BTN's range would be concentrated toward AK, A7, A4 strong holdings. In that version, Ad partially blocks AK combos - it becomes a positive blocker against value. Same card, opposite directional effect, because the turn line changed the composition of what BTN holds.

A.explanation: Against a competent regular, the Ad blocker reduces enough Ax bluff combos to push A8 below the thin-call threshold. This is a fold, though a close one.

B.explanation: Passive live players under-bluff ace rivers after free turns. Against a passive pool, A8 folds clearly even without the blocker analysis.

C.explanation: Aggressive recreationals overfire ace rivers after check-backs. Against a known overbluffer, A8 becomes a thin call even with the Ad blocker reducing some Ax-air combinations.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: ace blocker hurts in check-through spots - blocks bluffs, not value
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: Hero holds Ad on an As river after a turn check-through. Is the Ad a helpful blocker here - reducing BTN's value - or a harmful blocker - reducing BTN's bluffs?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: After a turn check-through, BTN's primary river bluffing region on the ace includes Ax-high delayed floats: A6, A5, A2, A9 that have no board connection and fire the scare ace as a narrative bluff. Ad removes all of those Ad-x combinations. It does not meaningfully block BTN's value (AK, A7, A4 type hands are present but not dominant after a check-through). Ad is a negative blocker - it reduces bluffs more than value, making the call worse than it appears.

options.label: Ad is always helpful - holding an ace reduces BTN's two-pair and strong ace hands.
After a check-through, BTN's delayed Ax-high air are the primary bluffs. Ad removes those combos - it blocks bluffs more than value. This is a negative blocker that makes calling worse.
Blockers at river are too small to affect the decision. Just count pot odds.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: blocker direction flips after bet-call
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: If BTN had bet the turn and BB called, how would Ad work as a blocker on the As river?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: After a turn bet-call, BTN's range is condensed toward strong holdings: AK, A7, A4 that bet the turn for value. In that range, Ad blocks some AK and A-x value combos - it becomes a positive blocker (reduces value). This is the opposite direction from the check-through scenario. Same card, opposite effect, because the turn line changed BTN's range composition.

options.label: Ad works the same way regardless of the turn action - it always blocks aces.

After a bet-call, BTN's range shifts toward AK and A-x value. Ad now partially blocks value combos - the direction flips from negative (blocking bluffs in check-through) to positive (blocking value in bet-call). Same card, opposite effect.
It doesn't matter - A8 folds to any river bet regardless of turn action.

coaching_context.key_concept: Blockers have direction that depends on the turn line. Ad after a check-through removes delayed Ax bluffs (negative blocker). Ad after a bet-call removes Ax value combos (positive blocker). The same card does opposite work depending on what the turn action did to BTN's range composition.
common_mistakes: Assuming holding an ace is always helpful because it 'blocks aces' - without specifying which part of BTN's range those aces appear in.
Applying blockers as absolute facts rather than as range-conditional effects.

value_buckets.label: Check-through value that bets As river
value_buckets.note: AK and strong K-x are the value core after check-through. Present but not dominant - the check preserved a lot of air.
value_buckets.frequency_hint: Moderate

bluff_buckets.label: Delayed Ax-high floats - primary bluff region after check-through
bluff_buckets.note: These are exactly what the Ad removes from BTN's range. Ax-high air that checked back K-7-4-3 and fires the scare ace river. Ad is a negative blocker against this constituency.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: High in check-through line - directly removed by Ad

bluff_buckets.label: Non-Ax delayed floats
bluff_buckets.note: These floats remain regardless of the Ad blocker. Their presence is what keeps A8 from being a trivial fold.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: Substantial - not blocked

bluff_catchers.label: A8 - borderline hand hurt by the Ad blocker
bluff_catchers.note: A8 is a thin bluff-catcher that sits at or just below threshold specifically because Ad removes its primary bluff constituency.
bluff_catchers.frequency_hint: Fold - the blocker is the marginal factor

threshold_notes: Without the Ad blocker, A8 might sit at threshold or slightly above in a check-through line.
The Ad removes ~8-12 Ax-high bluff combos from BTN's range, reducing the bluff-to-value ratio enough to push A8 below threshold.
In a bet-call line, Ad blocks value instead - A8 would still likely fold because the overall range is too strong, but the blocker direction is reversed.
blocker_notes: Ad blocks: A6d, A5d, A2d, A9d, A3d - Ax-high delayed floats that use As as a scare card. These are the primary bluffs in BTN's check-through range on the As river.
Ad does NOT meaningfully block: AK (only 3 combos, AKd specifically), K-x sets, and non-Ax delayed floats (QJ, T9s).
Direction flip: check-through (Ad blocks bluffs = negative) vs bet-call (Ad blocks Ax value = positive).

hero_hand_bucket.label: Pair of aces with bad blocker - below threshold
hero_hand_bucket.summary: FOLD - Ad removes the bluffs that would make this call correct
hero_hand_bucket.note: The lesson is blocker direction, not just blocker presence. Know which part of the range your blocker is working against.

what_changed_by_street.detail: 33% c-bet on K-7-4. Wide range remains. BTN has A-x floats, K-x value, backdoor draws, and all the delayed-float constituency.

3d blank. BTN checks back. The turn check-through preserves the full range - including the Ax-high air that will bluff the ace river. This is the setup that makes the Ad blocker directionally harmful.

As fires BTN's delayed Ax-high air as natural bluffs. Hero holds Ad - removing those combinations. The same hand that seems to 'have an ace' is actually holding the card that reduces the bluff density the call depends on.

coaching_context.follow_up: Study blocker direction systematically: when does holding the top card help (blocks value) vs hurt (blocks bluffs)? The controlling variable is whether the top card appears more in BTN's value range or BTN's bluff range for the specific line.

notes: BTN vs BB SRP river bluff-catch lane - Claude batch 4 - memory-decisive reconstruction spots
Blocker direction drill

title: KQ BB - Flush Blocker Direction Flips Based on Turn Line: Kc Hurts After Check-Through
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP. BTN c-bets 33% on Qc 9d 4h, then checks back the Ac turn after you check. River 7c completes the club flush. You check. BTN bets 70% pot. You hold Kc Qd. The Kc in your hand is working against you - not for you - after this turn check-through.

options.label: Fold
Call
Raise

answer.explanation: KcQd reaches the river with top pair and a flush blocker. The instinct is to call: 'Kc blocks the nut flush, so BTN has fewer nut-flush value combos.' That reasoning is valid when BTN's value range is flush-heavy - typically after a turn bet-call where BTN's two-club draws were in the barreling range. After a turn check-through, however, BTN's range still contains two-club combos, but more importantly, BTN's primary bluffing region on the flush-completing river includes hands like Kc-X where Kc completes a backdoor narrative without actually making the flush. Kc in hero's hand removes some of those Kc-bluff starting hands from BTN's range - specifically KcJx, Kc8x, Kc6x type hands that float the flop and bluff the flush river. The Kc is a negative blocker in this line: it reduces bluffs, not the value it appears to be blocking. If BTN had bet the Ac turn and BB called, the Kc would work in the positive direction - it would partially block AcKc, KcQc type made-flush value. Same card, different direction, because the turn line changed which side of the range the Kc sits in.

A.explanation: The Kc blocker is negative in a check-through line. KQ is top pair but loses to the flush, and the blocker analysis slightly worsens the case for calling. Fold, though this is close enough that a call is defensible against a competent bluff-heavy regular.

B.explanation: Passive live players rarely check back the Ac and then fire a flush river as a pure bluff. KQ folds clearly against a passive pool.

C.explanation: Aggressive recreationals overfire flush completers after free turns. Against a known overbluffer, KQ may be a thin call even with the Kc working as a negative blocker.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: flush blocker direction after check-through - blocks bluffs, not value
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: Hero holds Kc on a flush-completing river after a turn check-through. Is Kc reducing BTN's value or BTN's bluffs?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: After a check-through, BTN's value range on the flush-completing river is partially two-club made-flush hands, but a meaningful portion of BTN's bluffing region uses Kc-starting hands as backdoor-flush-narrative bluffs (KcJx, Kc8x). Kc removes those bluff starting hands from BTN's range. It also removes some AcKc and KcXc value combinations, but in a check-through line where the value region is broad (sets, two pairs), the relative effect on bluffs is larger. Kc is primarily a negative blocker in this line - it reduces bluffs more than value.

options.label: Kc blocks the nut-flush adjacent combos like AcKc. This always reduces BTN's value - Kc is a positive blocker.
After a check-through, BTN's bluff region includes Kc-starting float hands that fire the flush completion. Kc removes those bluffs from BTN's range - it is primarily a negative blocker in this line.
Flush blockers only matter when you are drawing to the flush yourself. Holding Kc with no flush has no blocker effect.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: Kc direction flips to positive after bet-call
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: If BTN had bet the Ac turn and BB called, how would Kc work as a blocker on the 7c river?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: After a bet-call on the Ac turn, BTN's range concentrates toward two-club holdings that were barreling the Ac: AcXc draws, KcQc, KcJc, and similar suited-ace combos. In that version, Kc partially blocks AcKc and KcXc made-flush value combos. The Kc now works as a positive blocker against value - the opposite direction from the check-through scenario.

options.label: Kc always blocks flush value regardless of the turn action. The direction doesn't change.

After a bet-call, BTN's range is concentrated toward two-club value hands. Kc now blocks AcKc and KcXc made flushes - it flips to a positive blocker against value. Opposite direction from check-through.
KQ folds regardless of turn action. Blockers are too small to matter in either version.

coaching_context.key_concept: Flush blockers change direction based on what the turn line put in BTN's range. After a check-through, a flush blocker can reduce bluffs more than value. After a bet-call, the same blocker primarily reduces made-flush value. Know which direction your blocker is working before using it to justify a call.
common_mistakes: Assuming a flush blocker always helps because it 'removes flush value' - without checking which side of the range it primarily removes given the specific line.
Calling river bets because 'I have a blocker' without identifying whether that blocker reduces bluffs or value.

value_buckets.label: Flush and strong value in check-through line
value_buckets.note: Sets, two pairs, and made flushes that checked back Ac with a trapping or pot-control plan.
value_buckets.frequency_hint: Broad value base after check-through - not flush-dominant

bluff_buckets.label: Kc-starting float bluffs - removed by Kc blocker
bluff_buckets.note: These float the flop and bluff the flush river with a Kc-narrative. Hero's Kc directly removes these from BTN's range - this is the negative blocker effect.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: Part of the bluff region directly blocked by Kc

bluff_buckets.label: Non-Kc delayed floats
bluff_buckets.note: These club draws and floats fire the flush river without needing Kc. Not affected by the Kc blocker.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: Substantial and unblocked

bluff_catchers.label: KQ - top pair with negative flush blocker
bluff_catchers.note: KQ loses to the flush and the Kc reduces the bluff density. Sits below threshold after the blocker analysis.
bluff_catchers.frequency_hint: Fold - negative blocker effect is the marginal factor

threshold_notes: KQ at 70% bet needs ~41% equity. Loses to all flushes, sets, and two pairs.
The Kc blocker removes some Kc-starting bluffs, reducing equity slightly below what a non-flush-blocker hand would have.
In a bet-call line, Kc would improve the case for calling by removing AcKc and KcXc value.
blocker_notes: Kc removes from BTN's range: KcJx, Kc8x, Kc6x as bluff starts; AcKc (1 combo) as value.
Net effect in check-through line: removes more bluff combinations than value combinations. Negative direction.
Net effect in bet-call line: removes AcKc, KcQc type two-club value. Positive direction.
The turn action determines which side of the ledger the Kc sits on.

hero_hand_bucket.label: Top pair with negative blocker - below threshold
hero_hand_bucket.summary: FOLD - Kc reduces bluffs in this line, not value
hero_hand_bucket.note: The drill targets the misconception that flush blockers are always positive. Direction depends on the line.

what_changed_by_street.detail: 33% c-bet on Q-9-4 with Qc. BTN's range includes all Kc-starting floats - KcJx, Kc8x - alongside value hands and two-club draws.

Ac arrives. BTN checks back. This preserves the full range including Kc-starting float hands that now have a backdoor flush story with Qc-Ac on board. The check-through sets up the negative-blocker scenario on the river.

7c completes clubs. BTN bets 70%. Kc-starting float hands now fire the flush as a narrative bluff. Hero holds Kc - removing those combinations from BTN's range. This reduces the bluff density enough to push KQ below threshold.

coaching_context.follow_up: Compare how flush blockers work in check-through lines vs bet-call lines on the same board family. Build the habit of assigning a direction to each blocker before using it to justify an action.

title: K7 BB - Multi-Step: Confirm the Turn Memory Before the River, Then Execute
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP. BTN c-bets 33% on Kd 8s 3c. The turn Jh arrived and something happened - before executing the river decision, confirm what the turn action was and why it matters. You hold Ks 7d.

options.label: Fold
Call
Raise

answer.explanation: The river decision follows directly from confirming the turn check-through in Step 1. Ks7d is top pair with a 7 kicker - low in the defending range. With a turn barrel, BTN's range contains AK, KJ two pair, JJ, and KJ-type value that makes a blank 4h river a fold. But the turn checked through. That preserves BTN's full delayed range including KQ and KT floats, J-high air, and missed straight draws (QT, T9, 97, 76) that now fire the 4h river as a bluff. K7 still beats all of those. It is a thin call but above threshold in the check-through scenario.

A.explanation: Thin call against a competent regular. K7 is low in range and the threshold is close, but the check-through justifies it.

B.explanation: Passive players under-bluff delayed lines. K7 with a 7 kicker is too thin to call against a passive pool.

C.explanation: Aggressive recreationals fire delayed lines frequently. K7 calls clearly in an aggro-rec pool.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: turn memory determines whether river call is correct
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: Before looking at the river card: if you learned that BTN had bet the Jh turn instead of checking, how does the river decision change for K7?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: A Jh barrel on K-8-3 represents AK, KJ, JJ, 88, 33 and strong draws. BTN's range is condensed. On a blank 4h river, K7 falls below threshold - top pair with a 7 kicker cannot call against a two-barrel value-heavy range. The turn check-through is what makes the river call viable.

options.label: It doesn't matter - K7 calls one river bet regardless of what happened on the turn.
A Jh barrel condenses BTN's range to KJ two-pair, JJ, AK, and strong draws. On a blank river, K7 falls below threshold and becomes a fold.
If BTN barreled, K7 should raise the river to take the pot away.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: K7 thin call after check-through
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: Given the turn checked through, is K7 above or below threshold for a 70% river bet on a blank 4h?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: After a check-through, BTN's range includes KQ/KT floats, J-high air, and missed straight draws that fire blank rivers as bluffs. K7 beats all of those and sits above threshold - though just barely. It is a thin call that is pool-dependent.

options.label: K7 is too weak regardless of the turn. Seven kicker means it should fold to any meaningful bet.
After a check-through, BTN's range has enough delayed bluffs that K7 sits above threshold for the 70% bet. It is thin and pool-dependent, but the call is correct in baseline and aggro pools.
K7 should lead-raise the river - it blocks some value and the paired board favors an aggressive line.

coaching_context.key_concept: Multi-street bluff-catching: the turn memory must be confirmed before the river execution. K7 is above threshold only because the turn checked through. Confirm the line, then act.
common_mistakes: Making the river decision without first confirming which turn action occurred - and ending up calling when a bet-call version would require a fold.
Folding K7 reflexively because the kicker is thin without checking whether the check-through preserves enough bluff density.

value_buckets.note: These trap through the turn or check for pot control. Some present but not dominant after check-through.
value_buckets.frequency_hint: Low to moderate

bluff_buckets.label: Delayed floats that fire blank rivers
bluff_buckets.note: Checked back the Jh turn and now fire the 4h blank as a bluff. K7 beats all of these.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: High density in check-through line

bluff_catchers.label: K7 - thin but above threshold
bluff_catchers.note: Low in the defending range. Above threshold in check-through scenario; below threshold after barrel.

threshold_notes: K7 is the approximate boundary of the defending range in this check-through scenario.
Against passive pools: fold - the bluff density does not materialize.
Against baseline and aggro pools: call - the check-through preserved enough air.
blocker_notes: Ks removes some KsKd (pocket kings) combinations - trivially positive but not material.
No meaningful blocker effect for K7 in this spot.

Fold - K7 is too thin even after a check-through

title: JT BB - Multi-Step Probe: Identify What Villain's Call Means, Then Fold the River
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP. After calling a 33% c-bet on Jh 7d 3c, you probe 45% pot on the 9s turn. BTN calls. Before acting on the 2h river, work through what that probe-call told you - then make the decision. You hold Jd Tc.

options.label: Fold
Call
Raise

answer.explanation: After working through the probe-call read in Step 1, the river decision becomes clear. BTN called a 45% probe on J-7-3-9. That call removed most of BTN's air and float range, retaining hands with genuine equity or board connection: KJ, QJ, JT (hero partially blocks with Jd), T8 straight draws that missed, TT, 88, and slow-played sets. On the 2h blank river, BTN bets 70% into a range they know is capped (hero probed turn, checked river). JdTc is top pair with a T kicker - one pair that loses to all of the two-pair, sets, and stronger J-x combos that called the probe. The T kicker also partially reduces some JT combinations in BTN's range. But the overall probe-call range is too strong for JT to call profitably. If the turn had checked through instead, JT would be a call - BTN's range would include real delayed floats. The probe destroyed that scenario.

A.explanation: A competent regular's probe-call range is tight and value-heavy. JT is well below threshold.

B.explanation: Passive live players almost never call probes with air. BTN's call range dominates JT. Clear fold.

C.explanation: Aggressive recreationals call probes with much wider hands including floats with backdoor draws, then fire river as bluffs at high frequency. Against a confirmed aggro-rec, JT is a thin call - their probe-call range retains more bluffing combos than other pools.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: probe-call range construction on J-7-3-9
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: After BTN calls your 45% turn probe on J-7-3-9, what does BTN's range look like reaching the river?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: BTN called a probe on a J-7-3-9 board. This removes most of BTN's air and weak floats. Remaining range: KJ, QJ, JT (blocked by hero), T8 straight draws with live equity, TT, 88, slow-played 77 and 33, and some gutshot-plus-overcard hands. This range dominates JT as top pair.

options.label: BTN can call probes with almost any holding - the range stays wide after a probe-call.
BTN's probe-call range is condensed toward board-connected hands: KJ, QJ, T8 draws, sets, and strong J-x. Most of the air folded to the probe.
BTN only calls probes with air or bluffs - value hands would have raised.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: check-through version would unlock river call
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: If the turn had checked through instead of you probing, would JT be above or below threshold for the 70% river bet?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: If the turn checked through, BTN's range would include the full delayed-float constituency: K-high air, Q-high floats, and backdoor draw misses. JT as top pair would beat those and sit above threshold for a bluff-catch call. The probe changed the problem - it removed those floats and invited BTN's strongest hands to continue.

options.label: JT folds regardless. One pair is too thin for any river bet on this board.
After a check-through, BTN has a wide delayed range that JT beats. The call is correct in that version. The probe is what changed it to a fold.
The probe was good - it got BTN to reveal their range, making the river decision easier.

coaching_context.key_concept: The multi-step structure teaches: identify what the probe-call told you first, then execute the river decision. JT is a clear fold once the probe-call range is understood. The check-through version would have been a call - the probe built this problem.
common_mistakes: Probing the turn with top pair and then treating the river decision as if the probe never happened.
Failing to reconstruct what BTN's probe-call range means before acting on the river.

value_buckets.label: BTN probe-call value on J-7-3-9
value_buckets.note: These hands called the probe with real equity or board control. They dominate JT on the river.
value_buckets.frequency_hint: Core value region after probe-call

value_buckets.label: Semi-bluffs with equity that called the probe
value_buckets.note: Straight draws with enough equity to call the probe. Some fire the river as a bluff after missing.
value_buckets.frequency_hint: Thin bluff population

bluff_buckets.label: Surviving equity hands that bluff blank river
bluff_buckets.note: Had equity on the turn, missed the 2h, fire as a bluff. Thin constituency - most air folded to the probe.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: Low

bluff_catchers.label: JT - below threshold after probe-call
bluff_catchers.note: Top pair with T kicker loses to all stronger J-x, two pairs, and sets that called the probe. Fold.

threshold_notes: JT is well below threshold in the probe-call line. The bluff fraction is too thin.
JT would be above threshold in a check-through line where the full delayed range is present.
The probe was the decision that changed the river threshold from call to fold.
blocker_notes: Jd removes one J from BTN's JT, KJ, QJ combos - marginally reduces value.
Tc removes some JT combinations - minor positive effect, not enough to change the decision.

9s arrives. BB probes 45%. BTN calls. This is the decisive event - not the river. The probe invited BTN's strongest hands to continue and removed the air. The river decision was built here.
2h blank. BTN bets 70%. Probe-call range is strong. JT is below threshold. Fold.

title: A8 BB — Exact-Match Transfer: Precisely Remembered Check-Through Produces Direct River Call
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP. BTN c-bets 33% on Ac 9d 5h, then checks back the 3c turn after you check. River Ad pairs the ace. You check. BTN bets 60% pot. You hold As 8h. This is an exact-match spot: you remember the turn precisely, the range logic transfers directly, and the call follows without reconstruction. Trips aces vs a check-through range that fires a paired river.

options.label: Fold
Call
Raise

answer.explanation: As8h makes trips aces after the Ad river (Ac on flop + Ad on river + As in hand). The turn check-through is precisely remembered — the player knows exactly what happened. That check-through preserved BTN's full delayed-float range: A-high air that uses the Ad as a scare-card bluff, K-high and Q-high floats that missed, backdoor draws that never developed. Trips is far above the threshold for any of those. The call here does not require reconstruction, threshold calculation, or sizing precision — it is a direct carryover from the exact line memory. This is what the exact-match assignment bucket looks like: precise line recall plus high-confidence hand strength equals direct transfer.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: identifying when a spot is an exact-match transfer
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: What makes this spot an exact-match transfer rather than a memory-reconstruction or sizing-stability problem?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: The player remembers the turn precisely (check-through confirmed), the hand strength is well above threshold (trips), and the river call follows directly without needing to reconstruct the line or calculate sizing thresholds. All three elements of exact-match transfer are present: certain line memory, confident hand position, direct range carryover.

options.label: Trips always calls on any river in any scenario — this is just a hand-strength decision, not a line-reading decision.
The turn check-through is precisely remembered, the hand is well above threshold, and the range logic transfers without reconstruction. All three exact-match conditions are met.
Even with trips, the 60% bet size needs to be verified against the threshold calculation before calling.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: identifying the decisive variable vs irrelevant uncertainty
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: In this exact-match spot, what is the decisive variable — and what uncertainties are completely irrelevant?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: The decisive variable is the turn check-through. It preserved the wide delayed-float range that makes BTN's river bet beatable. Irrelevant: the exact flop c-bet size (33% could have been 25-40% — doesn't change the turn check-through class), the exact river bet sizing (trips is above threshold at 60%, 75%, even 100%). The turn line is the only material fact.

options.label: The river bet size (60%) is the decisive variable. Different sizes would require recalculating pot odds.
The decisive variable is the turn check-through. It preserved the wide range that puts trips far above threshold. River sizing and flop sizing are both irrelevant.
The decisive variable is trips. Hand strength is the only thing that matters — the line is secondary.

coaching_context.key_concept: Assignment bucket: EXACT_MATCH. The player precisely recalls the turn check-through. Hand strength (trips) is well above threshold at any reasonable river bet. The range logic transfers directly from the known turn state. No reconstruction is needed and no sizing precision is required. This is what a direct-transfer spot looks like from the inside.
common_mistakes: Over-engineering the call by re-checking pot odds when the hand is obviously above threshold.
Treating trips on a paired-ace river as a slow-play candidate instead of a clear call.

value_buckets.label: Rare value in check-through range
value_buckets.note: Boats and quads that checked back the 3c turn with a trapping plan. Rare but present.
value_buckets.frequency_hint: Low — trips beats all of this anyway; same-or-better hand is not a concern here

bluff_buckets.label: A-high delayed floats firing the Ad river as a scare card
bluff_buckets.note: BTN held air Ax hands that couldn't call a check-raise on the A-flop. They check back the 3 turn and fire the Ad as a scare-card bluff.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: Primary bluff region in check-through line — trips beats all of these

bluff_buckets.label: Non-ace delayed floats
bluff_buckets.note: Checked back the turn and now fire the Ad as a narrative bluff using the scare card.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: Substantial — trips beats all of these

bluff_catchers.label: Trips aces — far above threshold
bluff_catchers.note: Trips are so far above threshold that the sizing (60%) is entirely irrelevant. Call immediately.

threshold_notes: At 60% pot: need roughly 37% equity. Trips wins against nearly all of BTN's check-through range.
At 100% pot: would need ~50% equity. Trips still wins easily.
Sizing uncertainty is completely irrelevant when the hand is this far above threshold.
blocker_notes: As removes Ash-X type combos from BTN's A-high bluff region — a minor positive effect.
Blocker analysis is unnecessary here; trips is already so far above threshold that blockers are irrelevant.

hero_hand_bucket.label: Trips aces — exact-match call
hero_hand_bucket.summary: CALL — direct transfer from precisely recalled check-through
hero_hand_bucket.note: The teaching target is recognizing when no reconstruction is needed.

what_changed_by_street.detail: 33% c-bet on A-9-5 high card board. BTN's full range stays in — all value, all floats, all delayed-float constituency.
3c blank. BTN checks back. Precisely remembered. This is the event that preserved the wide delayed range. The check-through is the controlling fact that will make the river call obvious.
Ad pairs the ace. Hero has trips. BTN's delayed range fires the scare ace as a bluff. Trips beats all of it. Call immediately. No reconstruction required.

coaching_context.follow_up: Study how to identify exact-match spots in real sessions: when is your line memory precise enough, and your hand strong enough, that no further analysis is needed?

title: 86 BB — Exact-Match Transfer: Precisely Remembered Turn Barrel Produces Direct River Fold
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP. BTN c-bets 33% on Kd 8c 5h, then bets 50% on the 3s turn after you check. You call. River Kh pairs the king. You check. BTN bets 75% pot. You hold 8h 6d. You remember the turn barrel clearly. The paired river makes the fold direct — no reconstruction needed.

options.label: Fold
Call
Raise

answer.explanation: 8h6d makes two pair (KK88) after the Kh river (Kd on flop + Kh on river + 8c on flop + 8h in hand = best five cards: Kd Kh 8c 8h 5h). The turn barrel is precisely remembered — BTN bet 50% on the 3s turn and BB called. That barrel condensed BTN's range toward AK, KQ, KJ, and set-type hands (88, 55, 33). The Kh river directly improves much of BTN's barrel range: AK becomes trips, KQ/KJ become trips, and any K-x holding is now stronger. Hero's two pair KK88 loses to all of BTN's trips and full houses. This is a direct-transfer fold: the precise turn barrel memory eliminates ambiguity about BTN's range at the river. No reconstruction is needed — the line is certain and the fold is downstream of that certainty.

A.explanation: Against a competent regular, a two-barrel line on K-8-5-3-K is value-heavy enough that two pair KK88 is below threshold.

B.explanation: Passive live players rarely fire two barrels without real strength. Two pair KK88 folds clearly against a passive pool.

C.explanation: Aggressive recreationals barrel wide and fire paired rivers as scare-card bluffs more often than other pools. Against a known aggro-rec overbluffer, two pair KK88 can be a thin call.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: exact-match transfer on a barrel-into-fold line
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: Why is this a direct-transfer fold rather than a reconstruction or memory-uncertainty problem?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: The turn barrel is precisely remembered. There is no uncertainty about the turn action class. The barrel condensed BTN's range toward K-x and strong holdings. The Kh river improves BTN's barrel range (AK to trips, KQ to trips). Hero's two pair KK88 falls below threshold against this condensed and improved range. The fold is a direct carryover from the known turn state.

options.label: Two pair is too strong to fold. KK88 beats enough of BTN's bluffing range to justify calling.
The barrel is precisely remembered. It condensed BTN's range toward K-x holdings that improved on the Kh river. KK88 is below threshold against that range. Direct-transfer fold.
The turn memory is uncertain enough that reconstruction is needed before folding.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: how the paired river improves the barrel range
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: The Kh pairs the king. How does this change BTN's barrel range on the river?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: BTN's barrel range on K-8-5-3 includes AK, KQ, KJ (K-high hands), 88, 55, 33 (sets). The Kh river improves AK to trips, KQ to trips, and KJ to trips. It also gives K8/K5 hands full houses. Hero's KK88 two pair loses to all of these. The paired river makes BTN's barrel range substantially stronger, not weaker.

options.label: The Kh is a scare card that BTN might bluff. The paired K could be a bluff trigger for missed hands.
BTN's barrel range includes AK, KQ, KJ. The Kh gives them trips. K8/K5 get full houses. The paired K river makes BTN's barrel range much stronger, worsening hero's equity.
The Kh is neutral — it doesn't help or hurt BTN's barrel range because everything already was K-high.

coaching_context.key_concept: Assignment bucket: EXACT_MATCH (fold version). The turn barrel is precisely remembered. The range logic transfers directly: BTN's condensed barrel range improved on the Kh river. Two pair KK88 is below threshold without reconstruction or sizing calculation needed.
common_mistakes: Treating two pair as a strong hand that auto-calls without checking what the barrel range looks like after a paired-K river.
Assuming the paired river creates a bluff opportunity for BTN when it actually improves BTN's barrel range.

value_buckets.note: These were BTN's primary barrel value on K-8-5-3. The Kh river gives them trips. All beat KK88.
value_buckets.frequency_hint: Core value region — dominant after barrel

value_buckets.label: Sets that improved

bluff_buckets.note: Straight draws that barreled with equity. Missed on river. Thin bluff residue but not enough to rescue KK88.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: Low — barrel compressed the bluff ratio

bluff_catchers.label: Two pair KK88 — below threshold after barrel
bluff_catchers.note: KK88 loses to all of BTN's trips and full houses. Below threshold even with partial blocker effect (8h blocks some 88 combos).
bluff_catchers.frequency_hint: Clear fold in all pools except confirmed aggro-rec overbluffers

threshold_notes: At 75% pot: need ~43% equity. KK88 does not reach that against a barrel range that improved on the Kh river.
The barrel filtered out most of BTN's air. The Kh river further improved the remaining value.
This fold is not pool-dependent in baseline or passive pools.

This minor blocker effect is insufficient to bring KK88 above threshold.

Kh pairs the king. BTN's barrel range (AK, KQ, KJ) gets trips. KK88 two pair is below threshold. Direct-transfer fold downstream of the precise turn barrel memory.

title: Q8 BB — Turn-Line Transfer: Knowing the Check-Through Family Is Sufficient for the River Call
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP. BTN c-bets the Qh 7s 4d flop (you remember a bet, size uncertain — could have been 25-40%). You called. Turn 6c: both players check. River Jd. BTN bets 65% pot. You hold Qd 8c. The exact flop c-bet size is fuzzy, but the turn line family (check-through) is certain. That family membership is what drives the river call — not the precise flop sizing.

options.label: Fold
Call
Raise

answer.explanation: Qd8c is top pair with an 8 kicker on Q-7-4-6-J. The decisive fact is not the exact flop c-bet size (uncertain between 25-40%) but the turn line family: the check-through is certain. That check preserved BTN's full delayed-float range — K-high and T-high air that missed, J-high hands that now use the Jd as a scare-card bluff trigger, 8-5 straight draws that missed, and Q-high floats that cannot represent value honestly. Qd8c beats all of those delayed bluffs. The exact flop c-bet size within the 25-40% range makes no material difference to the check-through range composition or to where Q8 sits relative to threshold. The turn line family (check-through) is the sufficient condition for the call — not any sizing precision. This is what turn-line transfer looks like: knowing the line family is enough.

A.explanation: Against competent regulars, Q8 is a thin call — it's at the lower boundary of the defending range. Call, but acknowledge it may be slightly below threshold against the tightest regulars.

B.explanation: Passive live players under-bluff delayed lines. Against a passive pool, Q8 doesn't have enough bluff density to justify the call and should fold.

C.explanation: Aggressive recreationals fire delayed lines frequently after check-backs. Against an aggro-rec, Q8 is a confident call — the bluff density is high enough to make it profitable.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: why flop sizing uncertainty is non-decisive when turn line family is certain
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: You're not sure if the flop c-bet was 25% or 40%. Does that uncertainty matter for the Q8 river call?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: The flop c-bet range within 25-40% is narrow enough that it doesn't materially change which hands BTN continues to the turn with. The turn check-through is the controlling event — it preserved the full delayed range regardless of whether the flop bet was 25% or 40%. The turn line family (check-through) is certain and sufficient; the flop sizing fuzz is non-decisive.

options.label: Yes — the flop c-bet size affects which hands BTN continues with, changing the turn check-through range composition.
No — within 25-40%, BTN continues with roughly the same hands. The turn check-through family is certain and that is the sufficient condition for the river call. Flop sizing fuzz is non-decisive.
Without knowing the exact flop size, the river threshold can't be calculated.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: check-through range on Q-7-4-6 arrives at Jd river with real bluffs
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: What specific hands in BTN's check-through range now fire the Jd river as bluffs, and why does Q8 beat them?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: The check-through preserved: J-high hands (KJ, QJ, JT) that now hit the Jd and bet for value OR fire it as a bluff; T-high missed straight draws (T8, 98) that failed to improve; K-high floats (KT, K9) that now use Jd as a narrative bluff. Qd8c — top pair — beats all of the air and bluff hands. It doesn't beat J-x value (QJ, AJ), but those are a minority of BTN's check-through range compared to the bluff constituency.

options.label: After a check-through, BTN rarely bluffs the river — the check-back usually means they have a strong hand.
The check-through preserved K-high air, T-high missed draws, and Q-high floats. The Jd river activates those as natural bluffs. Q8 beats all of them and is above threshold.
Q8 beats J-x hands like AJ and KJ, so the Jd river is good for hero.

coaching_context.key_concept: Assignment bucket: TURN_LINE_TRANSFER. The flop c-bet size is uncertain (25-40%), but the turn line family (check-through) is certain. Knowing the family is sufficient for the river call. This is what turn-line transfer looks like: the family membership, not sizing precision, drives the threshold.
common_mistakes: Refusing to call because the exact flop c-bet size can't be recalled, when the turn action class is the controlling variable.
Treating Q8 as too weak to call without running a precise pot-odds calculation when the check-through range is wide enough to make it profitable.

value_buckets.note: Some Jx hands checked back the turn and now bet for value on Jd river. Q8 loses to these.
value_buckets.frequency_hint: Low to moderate — not dominant after check-through on Q-7-4-6

bluff_buckets.label: Delayed floats that fire Jd river
bluff_buckets.note: Missed draws and high-card floats that use the Jd as a scare-card bluff trigger. Q8 beats all of these.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: High density — primary bluff region in check-through line

bluff_catchers.label: Q8 — at the lower boundary of the defending range
bluff_catchers.note: Above threshold in baseline and aggro pools; at or below threshold vs passive pools that under-bluff.
bluff_catchers.frequency_hint: Thin call — pool-dependent. Cleaner call at the Q9/QT level.

threshold_notes: At 65% pot: need roughly 39% equity. Q8 is above this against the check-through bluff density in baseline pools.
The flop sizing within 25-40% does not materially change this calculation.
The turn check-through family is the sufficient condition — not flop size precision.
blocker_notes: Qd removes one combo from BTN's QJ (Qd is in hero's hand) — minor positive effect on value density.
8c blocks some 8-5 straight draw combos — reduces a few bluff starts minimally.

hero_hand_bucket.label: Top pair — turn-line transfer call
hero_hand_bucket.summary: CALL in baseline/aggro pools; FOLD vs passive
hero_hand_bucket.note: The calling equity comes from the check-through family, not from hand strength alone.

what_changed_by_street.detail: C-bet (size uncertain, 25-40%) on Q-7-4. Wide range continues. The exact bet size within this band is non-decisive for the downstream river threshold.
6c blank. Both players check. This is the controlling event. The check-through is certain and it preserved BTN's full delayed range — the turn line family that transfers to the river answer.
Jd arrives. BTN fires. The check-through range's J-high floats, missed draws, and K-high air all activate. Q8 is above threshold. Call based on the family, not sizing precision.

coaching_context.follow_up: Study the boundary between flop sizing that is decisive vs non-decisive for the river threshold. At what point does flop sizing fuzz become material?

title: K5 BB — Turn-Line Transfer: Barrel Family Confirmed, River Polar Overbet Fold Direct
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP. BTN c-bets the Kc 8h 3s flop, then barrels the Ts turn. You're not sure if the turn bet was 40% or 60%, but you know BTN barreled. You called. River 4d blank. BTN overbets 110% pot. You hold Kd 5h. The barrel family is certain. The polar river overbet doesn't add new information — it confirms what the barrel already told you. Fold.

options.label: Fold
Call
Raise

answer.explanation: Kd5h is top pair with a 5 kicker on K-8-3-T-4. The turn barrel family is certain — BTN fired the Ts turn regardless of whether the size was 40% or 60%. That barrel condensed BTN's range toward AK, KQ, KJ, TT, 88, 33, and strong draws with the T. The exact turn bet sizing within 40-60% does not meaningfully change this range composition — both sizes keep the same hands. River 4d blank does nothing to help hero. BTN's polar overbet at 110% confirms a value-heavy or polar range (strong value and air, with the bluff fraction being thin after two barrels). K5 with a 5 kicker is well below threshold against any of this. The barrel family is the sufficient condition for the fold — not the exact turn size. At 110% river sizing, hero would need ~52% equity. K5 does not come close against a condensed two-barrel range.

A.explanation: K5 is well below threshold after a two-barrel line. Clear fold.

B.explanation: Passive live players almost never two-barrel and then overbet river without a strong hand. Fold clearly.

C.explanation: Against a confirmed aggro-rec who overbarrels and fires river overbets as pure bluffs, K5 may cross threshold as a thin call. Specific player read required.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: barrel family is sufficient condition for the fold — exact size irrelevant
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: You're not sure if the turn barrel was 40% or 60%. Does the sizing ambiguity affect the fold decision with K5?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: The barrel class is what matters — not the exact size. Both 40% and 60% turn bets keep the same core value hands (AK, KQ, TT, 88) and remove the same air. K5 is below threshold against the barrel range at either size. The sizing ambiguity is non-decisive for this specific fold because K5 is so far below threshold in any barrel scenario.

options.label: Yes — a 40% turn bet keeps more hands than 60%, which could affect whether K5 is above or below threshold.
No — within 40-60%, BTN keeps roughly the same core value hands. K5 is below threshold regardless. The barrel family class is the sufficient condition for the fold.
The river overbet (110%) changes the answer — overbets are bluffs and K5 should call.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: overbet context after a confirmed barrel — mostly value
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: BTN overbets 110% after a confirmed two-barrel line. What should this tell you about BTN's range composition?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: A polar overbet after two barrels is heavily weighted toward value with a thin bluff residue. The barrel already filtered out most of BTN's air. The river overbet on a blank 4d against a compressed calling range (BB called two streets) represents AK, KQ, and possibly TT who are value-betting large. Bluffs do exist but at a much lower ratio than in check-through spots. K5 is below threshold.

options.label: Overbets are typically bluffs — BTN is trying to fold K5 out. I should call to catch the bluff.
After two barrels, the bluff residue is thin. A river overbet here is heavily weighted toward value: AK, KQ, TT. K5 is well below threshold.
The overbet sizing doesn't tell me anything — BTN's range is the same regardless of how big they bet.

coaching_context.key_concept: Assignment bucket: TURN_LINE_TRANSFER. The barrel family is confirmed and sufficient. Exact turn sizing (40-60%) is non-decisive. The river overbet is downstream confirmation, not new information. K5 folds because the barrel class transferred — not because of the overbet size.
common_mistakes: Treating the river overbet as the main decision driver when the barrel already determined the fold.
Using turn size uncertainty as an excuse to delay the fold — the barrel class is confirmed and that's what matters.

value_buckets.label: Core barrel-and-overbet value
value_buckets.note: These hands barrel the T turn and overbet the river for max value. K5 loses to all of them.
value_buckets.frequency_hint: Dominant value region after two-barrel line

bluff_buckets.note: Semi-bluffs with equity that barreled and now fire river overbets as bluffs. Thin and insufficient for K5.

bluff_catchers.note: K5 is too weak to call any meaningful bet after a turn barrel. At 110% overbet: needs ~52% equity. Far below.

threshold_notes: At 110% pot: need ~52% equity. K5 is nowhere near this against a barrel range.

The river overbet is confirmation, not the primary driver of the fold.

title: J9 BB — Sizing-Stable CALL: Exact River Bet Size Is Fuzzy But the Call Is Stable at 50% or 70%
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP. BTN c-bets 33% on Jd 6h 2s, then checks back the 4c turn. River 8d. You check. BTN bets — you're not sure if it was around 50-55% or closer to 65-70%. You hold Js 9c. The exact size doesn't matter here. Top pair J-9 is above threshold at either end of that range after a check-through.

options.label: Fold
Call
Raise

answer.explanation: Js9c is top pair with a 9 kicker on J-6-2-4-8. The turn checked through precisely. The river bet is somewhere in the 50-70% range — the player can't remember the exact size. This is a sizing-stable spot: at 50% pot, hero needs roughly 33% equity; at 70% pot, hero needs roughly 41% equity. Js9c sits comfortably above the threshold at both ends of that range after a turn check-through. The check-through preserved BTN's full delayed-float range: K-high and Q-high air that uses the 8d as a bluff hook, T-high missed straight draws (T9, T7, 97, 75), and 9-8-7-6 connected hands that missed. Js9c beats all of those. The sizing uncertainty adds less than 8 percentage points to the required equity — an amount that does not change the call given the check-through range composition. Know the turn line precisely; accept the sizing fuzz as non-material.

A.explanation: Top pair J-9 is above threshold at 50-70% after a turn check-through. Call without needing to resolve the exact size.

B.explanation: Passive live players under-bluff delayed check-through lines. Against a passive pool, J9 may fall below threshold if the bluff density is low enough. Lean toward fold vs passive.

C.explanation: Aggressive recreationals fire check-through ranges at high frequency. J9 is a confident call in an aggro-rec pool regardless of the sizing ambiguity.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: sizing uncertainty is non-decisive when hand is above threshold at both ends
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: The river bet was 'around 50-70%' but you can't be more precise. Does that uncertainty change whether you call with Js9c?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: At 50%: need 33% equity. At 70%: need 41% equity. The difference is 8 percentage points. Js9c in a check-through range on J-6-2-4-8 is above 41% against the delayed-bluff constituency. The sizing uncertainty adds less than 8 percentage points to the threshold — not enough to flip the call. The band 50-70% does not straddle the threshold for this specific hand class.

options.label: Yes — 50% vs 70% creates meaningfully different pot odds that could change the decision.
No — J9 is above threshold at 50% AND at 70% in this check-through line. The 8-point difference in required equity doesn't straddle the threshold. Sizing fuzz is non-decisive.
Without knowing the exact size, default to folding to be safe.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: which delayed hands use 8d as a bluff hook
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: What specific hands in BTN's check-through range use the 8d river as a bluff hook, and why does J9 beat them?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: The check-through preserved T9, T7, 97, 75 straight draws that missed. It also preserved K-high and Q-high air without board connection. The 8d river gives T9 an open-ender completed, T7 and 97 partial connections, and K-high/Q-high air a scare-card narrative. Js9c (top pair) beats all of those pure bluffs. It does lose to value (J8s, 86s that made two pair), but those are fewer than the bluff constituency in a check-through range.

options.label: The 8d is a blank river and doesn't activate any meaningful bluff hands.
T9, T7, 97, 75 missed straight draws plus K-high and Q-high air use the 8d as a bluff hook. Js9c beats all of them and is above threshold.
J9 loses to all 8-x hands that hit the river — this should be a fold.

coaching_context.key_concept: Assignment bucket: SIZING_STABLE (call version). The sizing is fuzzy (50-70%) but J9 is above threshold at both ends. The check-through is the decisive fact; the sizing band is non-decisive. Know the turn precisely; accept acceptable sizing fuzz without hesitation.
common_mistakes: Using sizing uncertainty as an excuse not to call when the hand is above threshold at both ends of the uncertain range.
Treating J9 as too thin to call without exact pot odds when the check-through range is wide enough.

value_buckets.note: Slow-played Jx or two-pair hands that check turn and value-bet river. J9 loses to these but they are a minority.
value_buckets.frequency_hint: Low to moderate — not dominant in check-through range

bluff_buckets.label: Missed straight draws using 8d as a bluff hook
bluff_buckets.note: These missed improving on the 4c turn and now fire the 8d river as a bluff. Js9c beats all of them.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: High density — core of the check-through bluff region

bluff_buckets.label: K-high and Q-high air delayed floats
bluff_buckets.note: High-card hands without board connection that fire the 8d as a scare-card bluff.

bluff_catchers.note: Top pair with 9 kicker. Beats the full bluff constituency. Above threshold at both ends of the sizing band.

threshold_notes: At 50% pot: need 33% equity. J9 is well above this.
At 70% pot: need 41% equity. J9 is still above this against the check-through range.
The 8-point band (33-41%) does not straddle the threshold. Sizing fuzz is non-decisive.

9c removes some 9-x straight draw combos from BTN's bluff region. Minor positive blocker.

hero_hand_bucket.note: The check-through is the decisive variable; the sizing band is the irrelevant one.

4c blank. BTN checks back. This is the decisive event. The check-through preserved the full delayed range — straight draws, high-card air, and all float hands that will activate on a connected river card.

title: K4 BB — Sizing-Stable Fold: Ace River Overbet Range 90-120% Doesn't Change the Answer
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP. BTN c-bets Kh 8d 3c flop, then barrels the 6s turn. You called the turn with Kd 4h — top pair, garbage kicker. River Ah — an ace scare card. BTN overbets somewhere in the 90-120% range; you're not sure of the exact size. The turn barrel family is confirmed. The ace on the river makes the fold even clearer. The river sizing band is fuzzy but the answer is stable across the entire range. Fold.

options.label: Fold
Call
Raise

answer.explanation: Kd4h is top pair with a 4 kicker on K-8-3-6-Ah. The turn barrel family is confirmed — BTN fired the 6s turn and hero called. That two-barrel line condensed BTN's range toward AK, KQ, KJ, 88, 33, and semi-bluffs that improved (66). The river Ah is an ace scare card — it gives AK two pair and allows BTN to fire Ax hands as value or barrel the ace as a scare-card continuation. The ace makes K4's situation worse: AK now has two pair, and any Ax semi-bluff that arrived at the river now has a pair. BTN's overbet at 90-120% is fuzzy in exact sizing, but the fold is stable across the entire range. At 90% pot, hero needs ~47% equity. At 120% pot, hero needs ~55% equity. K4 does not approach either threshold. This is sizing-stable in the clearest sense: the ace arrival makes the fold even clearer, and no size in the 90-120% band changes that.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: Sizing stability when hand is well below threshold
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: BTN overbets somewhere between 90% and 120% pot. Does the exact sizing change your decision with K4?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: K4 needs ~47-55% equity depending on sizing. Against a two-barrel range, K4 has nowhere near that. The threshold is not crossed at either end of the band. Sizing uncertainty is irrelevant — the hand is too far below threshold.

options.label: No — K4 is too far below threshold at both ends of the sizing band. Fold regardless.
Yes — I need to know the exact size to calculate pot odds correctly before deciding.
Maybe — at 90% pot it's borderline, but at 120% it's a clear fold.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: Turn barrel vs check-through as the decisive variable
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: If BTN had checked back the 6s turn instead of barreling, how would that change the river decision?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: A turn check-through would expand BTN's range to include all missed draws and delayed floats that check back. K4 might be a marginal call in that scenario. The barrel family removed those hands and condensed BTN's range toward value. The turn line is the decisive variable — not the river sizing.

options.label: A turn check-through would meaningfully shift the range and potentially make K4 a call.
The turn action doesn't change the river decision — K4 folds to any overbet regardless.

coaching_context.key_concept: Assignment bucket: SIZING_STABLE (fold version). The river overbet is fuzzy (90-120%) but K4 is well below threshold at both ends. No calculation is needed once you confirm the turn barrel family. Sizing uncertainty is real but non-decisive.
common_mistakes: Treating sizing uncertainty as a reason to delay the fold — K4 is not borderline at any size in this band.
Confusing 'I don't know the exact size' with 'I can't decide' — the answer is stable even without size precision.

value_buckets.label: Value hands that fire two barrels on K-8-3-6-2
value_buckets.note: Strong kings plus sets and two-pair hands that barrel turn and overbet river for value.
value_buckets.frequency_hint: Dominant in two-barrel range — hero beats none of these

bluff_buckets.label: Semi-bluff draws that barrel and fire river as air
bluff_buckets.note: Gutshots or backdoor draws that barrel turn as semi-bluffs and fire river as air on the blank.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: Present but thin — not enough to pull K4 above threshold

title: KJ BB — Bridge Reconstruction: Confirm the Turn Barrel Before Trusting the River Answer
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP. You hold Kd Jd. Flop Ks 9d 4h. BTN c-bets, you call. Turn Th. You check. BTN bets — you recall calling the turn, but you're uncertain whether you're reconstructing the hand correctly. Before you can trust your river answer, you need to rebuild the turn story. River 9c pairs the board. BTN bets 65% pot. Work through the reconstruction, then decide.

options.label: Fold
Call
Raise

answer.explanation: KdJd on K-9-4-T-9 gives hero two pair: Kings and Nines (KK99J). The turn barrel was confirmed — the player called a BTN bet on the Th turn. The bridge reconstruction step is: confirm the turn was a barrel, not a check-through, before trusting the river read. Given the barrel, BTN's range entering the river is AK, KQ, TT, 99, some QJ/QJd draws, and air that bluffs two streets. The 9c pairing the board is important: it strengthens hero's relative position because hero holds KdJd (two pair), not just top pair. Meanwhile, bluffs that arrived at the river — QJ missed straights, diamond draws that missed — can still use the paired board as a scare card. Hero's KK99J two pair beats all of them. At 65% pot, hero needs ~39% equity. Two pair is well above that threshold even against a condensed barrel range. Call once the bridge reconstruction confirms the turn barrel.

A.explanation: Two pair KK99J is well above threshold at 65% pot after a barrel. Call.

B.explanation: Passive live players rarely barrel turn and then fire a second bet river without a strong hand. The bluff frequency in pool B is low enough that KJ two pair is close to threshold. Fold vs passive player who two-barreled.

C.explanation: Aggressive regs overbluff multi-street lines at higher frequency. KK99J two pair is clearly above threshold in this pool. Call and punish the overbluff.

Fold — not worth continuing with KJ

answer.explanation: Call. The Th turn barrel condensed BTN's range toward AK, KQ, TT, 99, and semi-bluffs like QJd, JXd draws. KdJd (top pair, J kicker with two diamonds) has enough equity to continue and is the right bluff-catcher hand to carry forward. Critically: recording this barrel in your reconstruction is the bridge you need. If you misremember this as a check-through, the river answer will be wrong.
