title: J8 BB — Bridge Reconstruction: Reconstructing the Barrel Makes the Fold Clear
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP. You hold Jc 8h. Flop Jd 9h 4c. BTN c-bets, you call. Turn Qs. You are reconstructing this hand from memory. You believe BTN bet the turn — you think you called, but you're not 100% certain. River 5s blank. BTN bets 70% pot. The first thing you need to do is confirm the turn line before trusting your river answer.

options.label: Fold
Call
Raise

answer.explanation: Jc8h is top pair with an 8 kicker on J-9-4-Q-5. The bridge reconstruction step is essential here: once you confirm BTN barreled the Qs turn and hero called, the fold becomes clear. The Qs turn barrel condensed BTN's range toward AQ, QJ, KJ, JJ, 99, 44, and straight draws like KT, T8 that picked up the queen. The 5s river blank does not help hero. BTN's river bet at 70% pot represents continued confidence in range strength. J8 with an 8 kicker is well below threshold against this condensed barrel range. At 70% pot, hero needs ~41% equity. J8 does not reach 41% against AQ, QJ value plus the thin bluff fraction. The reconstruction matters: if you misremember this as a BTN check-through, you might think J8 top pair is a call against a wider range. It is not. Confirm the turn barrel — fold is unambiguous.

A.explanation: J8 top pair weak kicker is below threshold after two barrels vs competent regulars. Clear fold.

B.explanation: Passive live players who barrel two streets on Q-broadway runout almost never bluff. J8 folds comfortably.

C.explanation: Aggressive recreationals overbarrel and bluff multiple streets without consistent value. J8 top pair is a thin call against pool C — their two-barrel bluff frequency is high enough to make the call profitable. Punish the overbluff.

diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: The Qs turn barrel removes BTN's pure air — K-high floats, underpair hands that didn't connect, and delayed floats that have no equity. What remains: AQ, QJ, KT-made straight, KJ, JJ, 99 for value, and semi-bluff draws like KT, T8 that picked up the queen and barrel as equity. J8 top pair is well below threshold against this condensed, value-leaning range.

Against that (incorrect) range, J8 top pair might look like a reasonable bluff-catcher. The bridge reconstruction prevents this error. Confirming the barrel is step one; the fold follows from that correct foundation.

The river decision is the same either way — J8 folds regardless of what happened on the turn.

title: A9 BB — Memory-Decisive: Turn Barrel Confirmed, River Answer Flips to Fold
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP. You hold Ac 9h. Flop As 8c 4h. BTN c-bets 33%, you call. Turn Ks. You check. BTN bets ~55% pot — you called. River 7d blank. BTN bets 70% pot. This is the memory-decisive question: which turn version happened? If BTN checked through, Ac9h is a river call. Because BTN barreled, it is a river fold. Confirm the barrel. Fold.

options.label: Fold
Call
Raise

answer.explanation: Ac9h gives hero pair of aces on A-8-4-K-7. The turn barrel is confirmed: BTN bet the Ks turn and hero called. That barrel condensed BTN's range toward AK (two pair), KK (set), and strong hands that connected with the K. The 7d river blank does not help hero. BTN's range entering the river is heavy with two-pair and set combinations. At 70% pot, hero needs ~41% equity. Pair of aces with a 9 kicker does not reach 41% against AK, KK, plus the thin bluff fraction. This is memory-decisive: had BTN checked through the Ks turn, BTN's range would include K-high delayed floats using the K as a scare card, missed draws, and all pure air that checked back. In that scenario, pair of aces with A9 is well above threshold and is a call. The turn barrel flip is the answer flip. Confirm the barrel, fold.

A.explanation: After a turn barrel on the Ks, pair of aces with 9 kicker is below threshold vs competent regs. Fold.

B.explanation: Passive live players who barrel a K turn almost always have it. Fold clearly.

C.explanation: Aggressive recreationals overbarrel K-runout as a scare-card bluff. Pair of aces with A9 is a thin call vs pool C's elevated bluff frequency. The memory-decisive flip applies in the other direction: their barrel doesn't represent the same condensed range.

title: T8 BB — Memory-Decisive: Check-Through Confirmed, Ace River Scare Card Calls
prompt: $2/$5 live. BB vs BTN SRP. You hold Td 8c. Flop Tc 7s 4d. BTN c-bets 33%, you call. Turn 2h blank. You check. BTN checks back. River Ad — an ace scare card arrives. You check. BTN bets 65% pot. This is the memory-decisive question: which turn version happened? Because BTN checked through, Td8c top pair is a river call — even vs the ace scare card. Had BTN barreled the blank turn, it would be a fold. Confirm the check-through. Call.

options.label: Fold
Call
Raise

answer.explanation: Td8c gives hero pair of tens on T-7-4-2-A. The turn check-through is confirmed: BTN checked back the blank 2h turn. That check-through preserved BTN's full delayed range — all A-high floats that now have A pair (and will bet the Ad as thin value or scare-card pressure), K-high and Q-high hands that use the Ad as a pure bluff hook, plus backdoor draws that never developed. The Ad arriving is a scare card — it genuinely upgrades A-high floats to A-pair. But in a check-through range, the PROPORTION of A-high delayed floats vs K-high/Q-high pure air is such that pair of tens is still above threshold. At 65% pot, hero needs ~39% equity. Even if ~35-40% of BTN's range has A-pair (which beats hero), the remaining 60-65% are K-high/Q-high bluffs that hero beats. Pair of tens has sufficient equity. This is memory-decisive: had BTN barreled the 2h blank turn, BTN's range would condense toward sets (77, 44) and strong draws. In that scenario, even the A-high delayed floats would have been bet out of range (most A-high hands don't barrel a blank without equity), and pair of tens would be well below threshold. The turn check-through is the flip that allows the call vs the scare ace. Confirm the check-through. Call.

A.explanation: Check-through range fires the Ad with A-high delayed floats (thin value) and K/Q-high scare-card bluffs. Pair of tens is above threshold against the combined range. Call.

B.explanation: Passive live players who check back the turn and then fire the Ad river often have A-pair or slow-played value. Their check-through-then-bet frequency is too low-bluff for T8 to call profitably. Fold vs pool B.

C.explanation: Aggressive recreationals use the Jd scare card at high frequency even from a check-through range. T8 top pair is a comfortable call and punishes the overbluff.

steps.prompt: Board: Tc 7s 4d. Turn: 2h blank. You hold Td 8c. You checked. BTN checked back. What does the turn check-through mean for BTN's range, and why is this the critical memory anchor for the river?

options.label: Proceed to river — check-through confirmed, range is wide
Probe the turn — take equity control

answer.explanation: Both players check the blank 2h turn. The check-through is the memory anchor: BTN chose not to barrel a blank turn, preserving their full delayed range — all missed draws, J-high floats, K-high and Q-high air that couldn't bet a blank turn but will use any face card on the river. Recording this: BTN checked back. This is what flips the river answer from fold to call.

what_changed_by_street.detail: C-bet 33% on T-7-4. Hero flats Td8c. Wide range on both sides. BTN has value, floats, and all straight draws.
2h blank. Both check. This is the decisive event. BTN's check-through preserved the full delayed range. This memory anchor determines the river answer.

coaching_context.why_preferred_line_works: Checking and taking a free river card is fine with top pair. The important outcome is confirming the turn check-through — this anchor makes the river call reliable.

hero_hand_bucket.label: Top pair — turn check-through confirmed
hero_hand_bucket.summary: Proceed to river with check-through anchor; call the river with T8
hero_hand_bucket.note: The check-through is the flip that makes T8 a river call instead of a river fold.

steps.prompt: River Ad — an ace scare card. Board: Tc 7s 4d 2h Ad. You hold Td 8c = pair of tens. BTN bets 65% pot. Turn check-through confirmed (step 1). Here is the memory-decisive contrast: barrel on the blank turn → FOLD. Check-through → CALL, even vs the ace scare card. Make the decision.

options.label: Fold — Jd is scary and pair of tens is thin
Call — check-through range fires the Jd with enough bluff density
Raise — represent a set or two pair

answer.explanation: Call. The check-through is confirmed. BTN's river range from a check-through line: A-high delayed floats that now have A pair (an upgrade that gives them thin value), K-high and Q-high hands that use the Ad as a pure scare-card bluff, and missed straight draws (8-6, 5-6, 9-8) that fire the ace. At 65% pot, hero needs ~39% equity. Even accounting for the A-pair hands in BTN's range, the K-high/Q-high bluff density from the check-through is high enough that T8 pair of tens is above 39% threshold. Contrast: if BTN had barreled the 2h blank turn, the A-high delayed floats would have been bet away (A-high without equity doesn't barrel a blank), leaving sets (77, 44) and strong draws — T8 pair of tens would be well below threshold. The turn check-through is the flip. Call.

what_changed_by_street.detail: Ad scare card arrives. BTN fires 65%. The check-through range activates A-high delayed floats (now A pair, thin value) and K/Q-high scare-card bluffs. T8 pair of tens is above threshold in the check-through range. The memory-decisive flip: check-through → call; barrel would have removed A-high floats and left only value → fold.

coaching_context.why_preferred_line_works: The river answer follows directly from the confirmed turn check-through. The confirmation step is what makes the call trustworthy — without it, T8 might look too thin vs any river bet.

hero_hand_bucket.label: Pair of tens — memory-decisive call (check-through version)
hero_hand_bucket.summary: CALL after check-through; would FOLD after barrel
hero_hand_bucket.note: Same hand, same river bet, different turn — different answer. This is what memory-decisive means.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: How the turn check-through vs barrel flips the river answer
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: With Td 8c on Tc 7s 4d 2h Ad, the river answer with the check-through confirmed is CALL. What would the answer be if BTN had barreled the 2h blank turn instead?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: A barrel on a blank 2h turn removes most delayed floats and air from BTN's range. What remains: sets (77, 44), strong top-pair-plus holdings, and committed semi-bluffs with equity. Against that condensed, value-heavy range, pair of tens with T8 does not reach 39% equity. The answer flips to FOLD. The check-through vs barrel is the decisive variable.

options.label: FOLD — a barrel on the blank turn condenses BTN's range toward value and committed draws. T8 pair of tens is below threshold.
Still CALL — pair of tens is strong enough regardless of what happened on the turn.
RAISE — if BTN barreled a blank turn and fires again, they're bluffing and I should raise.

diagnostic_prompts.concept: Why the Ad scare card still allows a call in a check-through range
diagnostic_prompts.prompt: BTN checked through the blank 2h turn and now fires the Ad river. The ace upgrades BTN's A-high floats to A pair (which beats hero). Why is pair of tens still a call?
diagnostic_prompts.expected_reasoning: In a check-through range, A-high delayed floats (A9, A8, A6 suited) now have A pair and bet as thin value. But BTN's check-through range also has large K-high and Q-high float constituency that uses the Ad as a pure scare-card bluff. The proportion of K/Q-high bluffs vs A-pair thin value is high enough in the check-through range that pair of tens is above 39% threshold. Hero loses to A-pair but beats the majority of the range.

options.label: Even with A-high floats upgrading to A pair, the K/Q-high scare-card bluffs dominate BTN's check-through range. T8 pair of tens is above 39% threshold.
The ace makes BTN's range too strong — A-pair beats T-pair. T8 folds.

coaching_context.key_concept: Assignment bucket: MEMORY_DECISIVE (call version). The turn check-through is confirmed — this is the flip from fold to call. Same hand (T8), same river bet (65%), different turn action, different answer.
common_mistakes: Folding to the Ad because 'an ace is always scary' — in a check-through range, the ace scare card is used as a bluff at high frequency by K/Q-high hands.
Folding pair of tens without confirming the turn action — the turn check-through is what makes T8 a call against the Ad.

value_buckets.label: A-pair value from check-through range that bets Ad river
value_buckets.note: A-high delayed floats that now have A pair. Hero loses to these — they upgraded on the river.
value_buckets.frequency_hint: Moderate — present in check-through range but not dominant

bluff_buckets.label: Scare-card bluffs using Ad
bluff_buckets.note: K-high and Q-high delayed floats that use the Ad river as a pure scare-card bluff hook. Hero beats all of these.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: High density in check-through range — core of the bluff constituency

bluff_buckets.label: Missed straight draws that fire the Ad
bluff_buckets.note: Straight draws on T-7-4 that missed improvement and fire the Ad as a bluff.
bluff_buckets.frequency_hint: Substantial — adds to bluff density

bluff_catchers.label: T8 pair of tens — check-through call
bluff_catchers.note: Beats the full bluff and scare-card constituency. Above 39% equity threshold at 65% pot in a check-through range.

threshold_notes: At 65% pot: need ~39% equity.
Pair of tens in check-through range (wide, bluff-heavy) is well above 39%.
Contrast: pair of tens vs barrel range (sets, strong value) would be well below 39%.
The threshold crossing is entirely driven by the turn action change.
blocker_notes: Td removes some AT combos from BTN's A-pair value range — minor positive effect.
8c blocks some 86s straight draw combos — minor reduction in bluff density. Still above threshold.

hero_hand_bucket.label: Pair of tens — memory-decisive call (check-through) / fold (barrel)
hero_hand_bucket.summary: CALL after check-through in baseline/reg/aggro pools; FOLD vs passive pool B; FOLD if barrel had happened
hero_hand_bucket.note: Pool B diverges because passive players rarely check-through and then bluff the river at meaningful frequency.

what_changed_by_street.detail: C-bet 33% on T-7-4. Hero flats Td8c. Wide range persists. BTN has all value, straight draws, and floats.
2h blank. Both check. This is the decisive event. The check-through preserved BTN's full delayed range. This is the memory anchor that flips the river answer to CALL.

Ad scare card arrives. BTN bets 65%. The check-through range activates A-high delayed floats (now A pair) as thin value and K/Q-high hands as pure scare-card bluffs. T8 pair of tens is above threshold in the check-through range. Call. Contrast: barrel on turn would have removed A-high floats (they don't barrel blanks without equity) and left only value — T8 would then fold.

coaching_context.follow_up: Practice the memory-decisive contrast: replay this hand with BTN barreling the 2h turn. Walk through the fold in that version. Make the two versions vivid — same hand, same river bet, different answer. That contrast IS the assignment bucket.
